Intel, HP, and Micron are cyclical tech names that could benefit from a recovery in PC and semiconductor demand. Cloud stocks that have lagged the mega-cap rally may be bargains. Insider selling at year-end is typical and not necessarily bearish. The Santa Claus Rally has delivered, and the market is poised to close the year on a high note. Looking ahead to 2024, the key variables are the pace and timing of Fed rate cuts, the trajectory of inflation, the health of the consumer, and the outcome of the US presidential election. Corporate earnings will need to grow to justify current valuations. The market may see a pullback in January as investors take profits, but the overall trend remains positive. Investors should focus on quality, diversify across sectors and geographies, and keep an eye on the 10-year Treasury yield. The AI theme will continue, but selectivity is crucial. The year ahead promises volatility, but also opportunity. As always, patience and discipline will be rewarded.”
